subject: Sociology
title: Sociology and Social Movements
description: One-minute skill: Sociology and Social Movements.

hook.text: Rosa Parks didn't just sit down. She was trained, organized, and strategic. Movements look spontaneous but rarely are.

buildup.text: Social movements are collective efforts to promote or resist change. They require organization, not just anger.

discovery.text: Resource mobilization theory says movements succeed when they gather money, media access, and committed leaders.

twist.text: Grievances alone don't create movements. Millions suffer in silence. Movements need structure to turn pain into action.

climax.text: From civil rights to climate strikes, movements reshape societies — but only when organization meets opportunity.

punchline.text: Revolutions aren't spontaneous. They're organized and timed.

quiz.question: What does resource mobilization theory emphasize?
options: Movements need organization, money, and leadership to succeed
Anger alone is sufficient to create change
Social media is the only driver of modern movements
